summary: A densely filled test manuscript record for testing long-list rendering and show-more behavior. Three-part composite codex: Part 1 contains a 10th-century Arabic Synaxarion with a full monthly contents list; Part 2 contains a 12th-century Arabic Horologion with multiple text units; Part 3 contains a 13th-century Arabic liturgical supplement with guest content and extensive paracontent.
coll: Quire 1: 1x8 (8); Quire 2: 1x8 (16); Quire 3: 1x8 (24); Quire 4: 1x8 (32); Quire 5: 1x8 (40); Quire 6: 1x8+1 (49); Quire 7: 1x8 (57); Quire 8: 1x8 (65)

part.label: Part 1
part.summary: Arabic Synaxarion (Menologion), organized by month; 10th century, Naskh script on parchment

para.as_written: وقف هذا الكتاب على دير القديسة كاترينا
translation: This book is a pious endowment to the Monastery of Saint Catherine

note.value: Fine parchment throughout; hair and flesh sides clearly distinguishable
Regular quires of 8 throughout; no catchwords

related_mss.label: Related Synaxarion overtexts
note: Overtext tradition shared with Sinai Arabic 400 and 402

related_mss.label: Continuation quires now in Sinai Arabic NF X 300
note: Months Teshrin I through Adar are continued in Sinai Arabic NF X 300

layer_record.label: Sinai Arabic NF X 299, Part 1, Overtext
layer_record.summary: 10th-century Arabic Synaxarion arranged by the Syriac calendar, Naskh script, with rubrics and decorated headpieces at each monthly division.

note: Formal Naskh throughout; heading script larger and more carefully formed
Vowel pointing used inconsistently; more frequent in liturgical sections
Some corrections in a second, slightly later hand visible throughout
Kufic elements in the opening decorative headpiece inscription
Main text in brown iron gall ink
Red used for monthly headings, feast day rubrics, and section markers throughout
Polychrome decoration in opening headpiece
Gold used for the central inscription panel
Blue and green in the geometric border elements
Ruled in drypoint
Text written below top line throughout
Outer margin reserved for reader annotations
Layout shifts to two columns in the final monthly sections
Line count increases slightly in the final quires

text_unit_record.label: Arabic Synaxarion
text_unit_record.summary: A complete Arabic Synaxarion arranged according to the Syriac calendar, providing liturgical readings for saints' feasts and commemorations for each month of the year.

work.desc: A liturgical book containing brief lives of saints and commemorations arranged according to the calendar, used in Eastern Christian churches.
note: The Arabic Synaxarion tradition is closely related to the Melkite liturgical tradition

cataloguer.message: Description and identification of undertexts as part of SPP (2011-2016). Migrated to SMDL in 2025.

excerpt.as_written: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم كتاب السنكسار المبارك المرتب على شهور السنة السريانية
translation: In the name of God the compassionate and merciful: the blessed Synaxarion arranged according to the months of the Syriac year

excerpt.as_written: في أول يوم من شهر أيلول تذكار القديس سمعان عمود النساك
translation: On the first day of the month of Elul, commemoration of Saint Simeon, Pillar of the Ascetics

excerpt.as_written: تمّ كتاب السنكسار المبارك بعون الله وقوته
translation: The blessed Synaxarion is completed by the grace of God and his power

note: Arrangement follows the Syriac calendar beginning with Elul
Each monthly section opens with a decorated headpiece
Text tradition closely related to the Melkite Synaxarion

bib.citation: Graf, Georg. Geschichte der christlichen arabischen Literatur. Vatican City: Biblioteca Apostolica Vaticana, 1944.

para.as_written: كتب هذا السنكسار المبارك بيد الخاطئ الراهب يوحنا في دير القديسة كاترينا بجبل سيناء في شهر أيلول سنة ألف ومئتين وخمسة وعشرين
translation: This blessed Synaxarion was written by the hand of the sinful monk Yuhanna at the Monastery of Saint Catherine on Mount Sinai in the month of Elul in the year 1225

note: Named in the colophon
